tool.name: scripts/generation_mode_gate.py
tool.version_ref: Project Theseus local working tree reviewed 2026-07-01; tool sha256 e99477a1b9546c14c60dc8e2b442f1437274d7ba367e717c23b608fb41fd290b

input_class: Project Theseus generation-mode registry over decode, fanout, verifier, VCM, and tool-assisted completion accounting; imported as a static public-safe summary, not rerun from this repository.

source_artifacts.sanitization_summary: The ASI-side fixture records summary counts, boundary gates, comparison decisions, rules, digests, residuals, and non-claims. It does not copy task rows, prompts, candidate outputs, model checkpoints, training rows, private traces, or benchmark payloads.
The config digest identifies the reviewed registry. The ASI-side fixture does not vendor the full Project Theseus config.
The tool digest identifies the reviewed gate implementation. The ASI-side fixture does not vendor Project Theseus tool code.

rules.speed_claim: Speed claims must report accepted spans per second and useful verified solutions per second separately.
rules.promotion: A faster mode is not promotable if verifier pass, integrity, context adequacy, no-cheat counters, or fallback burden regresses.
rules.kv_claim: VCM descriptor reuse is not native model KV/prefix-cache parity unless a model-runtime lifecycle test passes.
rules.learned_generation: Generation-mode acceleration does not bypass candidate-integrity or learned-generation claim rules.

failed_attempts.reason: The local Project Theseus checkout contained modified and untracked files, including private-data surfaces, so this ASI Stack increment imports only a static sanitized report digest and does not claim a fresh live rerun.

residuals: The ASI Stack repository verifies the public-safe fixture and source-artifact digests; it does not run Project Theseus generation-mode commands in CI.
The source checkout was dirty at import review, so the static report digest is evidence of a checkpointed local report, not a current Theseus replay.
The imported report shows useful solution per second remained zero and no comparison was promotable, so it is negative evidence against raw-speed promotion rather than a speed-quality success.
Chapter-core claims remain at argument until a separate evidence-transition record is accepted.

replay.verifier: ASI Stack validator checks schema, source digests, boundary gates, negative comparison count, zero promotable comparisons, useful-solution boundary, mutation controls, redaction boundary, result digest, and non-claims.

claim_boundaries: This report can be cited as public-safe implementation-reference evidence that a Project Theseus checkpointed generation-mode gate recorded 18 modes, 13 comparisons, zero hard gaps, five hard boundary gates passing, and zero promotable comparisons.

This report is negative evidence against raw-throughput promotion: five comparisons saw accepted-span speed lift but every candidate task-pass count stayed zero and useful solution per second stayed zero.
This report cannot by itself promote generation-speed, model-quality, routing-quality, benchmark, resource-economics, Project Theseus, or ASI claims.
A future promotion would need a clean replay, public task bundle or archived fixture, quality and residual review, negative controls, and an accepted evidence-transition record.
non_claims: does not promote any chapter core claim above argument
does not prove generation speed, useful-solution-per-second improvement, model quality, routing quality, benchmark quality, safety, alignment, transfer, or ASI
does not authorize heavy training inside The ASI Stack book or reproduce the Project Theseus generation-mode pipeline
does not copy private task rows, prompts, candidate outputs, training rows, checkpoints, traces, or benchmark payloads into this public repository
does not replace future clean Project Theseus replay, public task bundle, external review, or chapter-core evidence-transition review
